title: Visual Mode
intro: `v` starts Visual mode: motions extend the selection, then an operator acts on it. `V` selects whole lines. Text objects work here too: `vaw` selects a word.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on `remove`. Select the word with `vaw`, then delete the selection with `d`.
hints: `v` `a` `w` selects a word plus its trailing space; `d` deletes the selection.

exercises.instruction: Select the whole middle line with `V` and delete it with `d`.
The cursor is on the comment. Select to the end of the line with `v$` and delete it.
hints: Start Visual with `v`, extend with `$`, then `d`.

exercises.instruction: Select both middle lines with `V` then `j`, and delete them with `d`.
hints: In line-wise Visual mode, `j` extends the selection down.

exercises.instruction: Delete the comment visually: jump onto the `/` with `f/`, select to the end of the line with `v$`, then delete with `d`.
Select all three middle lines with `V` `j` `j`, then delete them with `d`.
hints: `V` starts line-wise Visual mode; each `j` pulls one more line into the selection.
